(married name **Natalie Chestney**, born 28 July 1989 in [Torquay](/wiki/Torquay "Torquay")) is a [lawn bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") from [Newton Abbot](/wiki/Newton_Abbot "Newton Abbot").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

[THE ENGLAND TEAM | Sporting Life | Commonwealth Games, Delhi 2010, Medal Table](http://www.sportinglife.com/commonwealth2010/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=others/10/09/28/manual_172154.html) [Archived](https://web.archive.org/web/20111025092904/http://www.sportinglife.com/commonwealth2010/news/story_get.cgi?STORY_NAME=others/10/09/28/manual_172154.html) 25 October 2011 at the [Wayback Machine](/wiki/Wayback_Machine "Wayback Machine")

["Past Records"](https://web.archive.org/web/20190327221853/https://www.bowlsengland.com/about-us/past-records). Bowls England. Archived from [the original](https://www.bowlsengland.com/about-us/past-records) on 27 March 2019. Retrieved 23 January 2018.

["Previous Winners"](http://www.biwbc.com/community/british-isles-women-s-bowls-council-15075/home). British Isles Bowls Council.[*[permanent dead link](/wiki/Wikipedia:Link_rot "Wikipedia:Link rot")*]

**Natalie Melmore** (married name **Natalie Chestney**, born 28 July 1989 in [Torquay](/wiki/Torquay "Torquay")) is a [lawn bowler](/wiki/Bowls "Bowls") from [Newton Abbot](/wiki/Newton_Abbot "Newton Abbot").[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Bowls career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Natalie_Melmore&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Bowls career")]

Natalie represented England at the [2010 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2010_Commonwealth_Games "2010 Commonwealth Games") where she won a gold medal in the [women's singles](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2010_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_singles "Lawn bowls at the 2010 Commonwealth Games – Women's singles") event.[[2]](#cite_note-Gold-2) In 2011 she won the singles and triples silver medals at the [Atlantic Bowls Championships](/wiki/Atlantic_Bowls_Championships "Atlantic Bowls Championships").[[3]](#cite_note-3)

Natalie went on to win a further two silver medals in the [2014 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2014_Commonwealth_Games "2014 Commonwealth Games") in the [women's singles](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2014_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_singles "Lawn bowls at the 2014 Commonwealth Games – Women's singles") and [women's pairs](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2014_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_pairs "Lawn bowls at the 2014 Commonwealth Games – Women's pairs") events with partner [Jamie-Lea Winch](/wiki/Jamie-Lea_Winch "Jamie-Lea Winch").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In 2016 she secured a fourth and fifth [National title](/wiki/List_of_Bowls_England_Women%27s_National_Championships "List of Bowls England Women's National Championships") win[[4]](#cite_note-4) and in 2018 was part of the fours that won the National Championships.[[5]](#cite_note-5) After her 2014 singles success she subsequently won the singles at the [British Isles Bowls Championships](/wiki/British_Isles_Bowls_Championships "British Isles Bowls Championships") in 2015.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

She was selected as part of the [English team](/wiki/England_at_the_2018_Commonwealth_Games "England at the 2018 Commonwealth Games") for the [2018 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2018_Commonwealth_Games "2018 Commonwealth Games") on the [Gold Coast](/wiki/Gold_Coast,_Queensland "Gold Coast, Queensland") in [Queensland](/wiki/Queensland "Queensland").[[7]](#cite_note-7) In 2019 she won the singles and pairs bronze medals at the Atlantic Bowls Championships.[[8]](#cite_note-8)

In 2022, she competed in the [women's triples](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_triples "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Women's triples") and the [Women's fours](/wiki/Lawn_bowls_at_the_2022_Commonwealth_Games_%E2%80%93_Women%27s_fours "Lawn bowls at the 2022 Commonwealth Games – Women's fours") at the [2022 Commonwealth Games](/wiki/2022_Commonwealth_Games "2022 Commonwealth Games").[[9]](#cite_note-9) She won the gold medal in the triples with Jamie-Lea Winch and [Sian Honnor](/wiki/Sian_Honnor "Sian Honnor").[[10]](#cite_note-10)

## Personal life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Natalie_Melmore&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Personal life")]

In 2015, she married [Jamie Chestney](/wiki/Jamie_Chestney "Jamie Chestney").[[11]](#cite_note-11)
